item.title: Messi to captain Argentina at the World Cup
item.description: Lionel Messi will captain Argentina at the World Cup, national coach Lionel Scaloni announced Thursday as he unveiled his 26-man squad....

item.title: 'Six machine' Sooryavanshi, 15, stakes India claim with new stunning knock
item.description: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's latest extraordinary innings was hailed by cricket greats on Thursday, as calls grow for the 15-year-old phenomenon to be propelled straight into the senior India team....

item.title: Son 'feeling great' for World Cup despite goal drought
item.description: Son Heung-min said he was ‘feeling great’ ahead of the World Cup and backed himself to perform despite suggestions that South Korea's talismanic captain's powers are on the wane....

item.title: Scaloni encouraged by news on Messi injury
item.description:
Argentina coach Lionel Scaloni said the initial news regarding superstar Lionel Messi's fitness ‘is not that bad’ and hopes further examinations will confirm that diagnosis with the World Cup just a fortnight away.
...

item.title: Giant Messi statue in India to be removed over safety concerns
item.description: A giant statue of football superstar Lionel Messi erected in India to mark his visit last year is ‘swaying in the wind’ and will be removed, a lawmaker told AFP on Wednesday....

item.title: Amorim axe costs Manchester United £16.7 million
item.description: Manchester United's decision to sack head coach Ruben Amorim and his staff with 18 months left on the Portuguese's contract cost the Premier League giants £16.7 million ($22.5 million), according to a club financial statement published on Wednesday....

item.title: Depay in Netherlands World Cup squad, Frimpong misses out
item.description:
Netherlands coach Ronald Koeman has included Corinthians forward Memphis Depay in his World Cup squad announced on Wednesday, but Liverpool wing-back Jeremie Frimpong misses out.
...

item.title: Germany urge players to avoid political statements at World Cup
item.description: Germany sporting director Rudi Voeller on Wednesday urged members of the World Cup squad to avoid making political statements during this summer's tournament....

item.title: Yamal's Spain dreaming of Euro-World Cup double repeat
item.description: Boosted by teenage phenomenon Lamine Yamal, Luis de la Fuente's Spain are attempting to follow in the footsteps of the country's golden generation, who conquered Europe in 2008 and then the world two years later....

item.title: Iran expects FIFA to facilitate multi-entry visa to US for team
item.description: Iran's football federation expects FIFA to facilitate multiple-entry visas for the United States for the Iranian national team ahead of the 2026 World Cup, its president said in a video broadcast by Iranian media Thursday....

item.title: Neymar a doubt for Brazil's World Cup opener
item.description:
Brazil forward Neymar is a doubt for the start of the World Cup after injuring his calf, requiring two to three weeks of treatment, the national team's doctor Rodrigo Lasmar said on Thursday.
...

item.title: Online child protection likely to find favour at digital G7
item.description: Ministers gathering for a digital G7 meeting in Paris on Friday will likely find common ground on online child protection,...

item.title: Two youths killed in bus–CNG collision in Mymensingh
item.description: Two young men were killed and another seriously injured after a bus collided head-on with a CNG-run auto-rickshaw in Mymensingh on Eid night....

item.title: Modi extends Eid greetings to Tarique Rahman
item.description: Indian prime minister Narendra Modi has conveyed warm greetings to prime minister Tarique Rahman and the people of Bangladesh on the occasion of holy Eid-ul-Azha....

item.title: US, Iran agree deal framework, await Trump approval: US source
item.description: US and Iranian negotiators have agreed on a framework for a 60-day ceasefire extension deal but it still needs US president Donald Trump’s approval, US sources said......

item.title: Tk 18 crore toll collected on Padma Bridge in four days
item.description: A total of 160,674 vehicles crossed the bridge through the Mawa and Zajira ends, generating toll revenue of Tk 18,04,08,200 in four days last as Eid holidaymakers from 21 southern districts returned to their village homes without major inconvenience.......

item.title: Bangladesh celebrates Eid-ul-Azha
item.description: Eid-ul-Azha, the second largest religious festival of Muslims, was celebrated across Bangladesh with due religious fervor and festivity on Thursday.......

item.title: Four killed in separate road accidents in Gaibandha
item.description: Four people were killed in separate road accidents in Gobindaganj and Sundarganj upazilas of Gaibandha on Thursday......

item.title: Bangladesh celebrating Eid-ul-Azha with religious fervour
item.description: Holy Eid-ul-Azha, the largest religious festival of Muslims, is being celebrated in Bangladesh with religious fervour and festivity on Thursday....

item.title: US climber smashes Everest speed record
item.description: US climber Tyler Andrews set a new speed record using supplementary oxygen on Mount Everest on Thursday, his team told AFP, summiting the world’s highest peak an hour quicker than anyone before.......

item.title: Huge crowd at national zoo to see albino buffalo ‘Donald Trump’
item.description: On Thursday, a huge number of visitors entered the zoo to see the buffalo, according to Atiqur Rahman, the curator of the zoo......

item.title: Israel breaks all contact with UN secretary-general
item.description: The Israeli mission to the United Nations said in a statement that it would have no contact with the secretary-general’s office as long as Guterres serves as its head......

item.title: Bangladesh president, PM offer Eid prayers at National Eidgah
item.description: Bangladesh president Mohammed Shahabuddin and prime minister Tarique Rahman offered Eid-ul-Azha prayers at the country’s main congregation at the National Eidgah on Thursday morning....

item.title: India orders demolition drive along Pakistan border
item.description: India’s interior minister has ordered the demolition of illegal buildings within 15 kilometres (nine miles) of the border with arch-enemy Pakistan, his office said on Wednesday.......

item.title: US to halt Iran airlines’ access to landing spots
item.description: In a post on X, Bessent said that Washington will ‘be shutting down both Iranian airlines’ access to landing spots, refueling, and ticket sales,’ without giving details......

item.title: US conducts new strike in south Iran: official
item.description: The US military shot down four Iranian drones and struck a control centre in the southern city of Bandar Abbas, an American official said Wednesday, describing the actions as ‘purely defensive.’...

item.title: City authorities to clear sacrificial animal waste by early Friday: state minister
item.description: All city corporations in Bangladesh have been instructed to complete the removal of sacrificial animal waste by early Friday, state minister for local government, rural development and cooperatives Mir Shahe Alam said on Thursday.......

item.title: Kenya girls’ school hostel fire kills 16
item.description: Children have been accused of deliberately starting school fires in Kenya in the past. One report found there were 63 arson cases at schools in 2018 alone......

item.title: BNP’s Rizvi meets slain Ramisa’s family on PM’s behalf on Eid day
item.description: Prime minister’s adviser and BNP senior joint secretary general Ruhul Kabir Rizvi on Thursday met the family of slain child Ramisa Akter in their Pallabi home in Dhaka on the day of Eid-ul-Azha.......

item.title: US, Iran trade strikes in most serious clash since truce began
item.description: The latest exchange also drew in US ally Kuwait, which said it was responding to incoming fire, and came as violence on the war’s Lebanese front escalated sharply......

item.title: Measles: Five more children die in 24 hours
item.description: Five more children died from measles and similar symptoms in Bangladesh in the 24 hours till 8:00am on Thursday, bringing the total death toll to 565 since March 15, 2026.......

item.title: President calls for building prosperous Bangladesh
item.description: President Mohammed Shahabuddin on Thursday called upon all to work together for building a just, democratic, non-communal and prosperous Bangladesh imbued with the ideals and values of Eid-ul-Azha.......

item.title: BNP for building a Bangladesh free from discrimination
item.description: The Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Thursday has called for building a Bangladesh free from discrimination.......

item.title: Two minor children killed in Dinajpur road accident
item.description: Two children were killed and three others including their mothers were injured after a CNG-run auto-rickshaw crashed into a truck in Kanagari Bazar highway area of Dinajpur’s Ghoraghat upazila early Thursday.......

item.title: PM Tarique Rahman visits parents' graves, offers prayers
item.description: Bangladesh prime minister and BNP chairman Tarique Rahman on Thursday visited the graves of his parents at Zia Uddyan in the capital and offered prayers on the occasion of the Eid-ul-Azha.......

item.title: Five killed in Gopalganj road accident
item.description: At least five people were killed and 20 others were injured in a head-on collision between a bus and a motorcycle on the Dhaka–Khulna Highway in the Bedgram area of Sadar upazila in Gopalganj district on Thursday afternoon........

item.title: PM visits graves of army officers killed in BDR carnage
item.description: Prime Minister Tarique Rahman on Thursday visited the graves of army officers killed in the 2009 BDR mutiny at Banani Military Graveyard....

item.title: Eid-ul-Azha: Nearly 1 lakh devotees attend Sholakia Eid congregation despite heavy rain
item.description: Defying heavy rain and gloomy weather, nearly one lakh devotees joined the 199th Eid-ul-Azha congregation at the historic Sholakia Eidgah Maidan in Kishoreganj on Thursday under a high security arrangement....
